commands.review_summary:
The branch removes `speech-core` from bundled-extension runtime facade staging/activation allowlists and adds npm postpublish verification for always-allowed runtime facades.

PR surface: Source -2, Tests +80, Other +133. Total +211 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main still allows `speech-core/runtime-api.js` as a bundled runtime facade while the supported TTS runtime path imports `packages/speech-core/runtime-api.js`, matching the linked installed-package failure mode.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because no blocking review finding was found; the remaining action is normal CI and maintainer merge handling.

target.title: fix #89425: [Bug]: Missing extensions/speech-core/ in npm tarball (v2026.5.28) — "Unable to resolve bundled plugin public surface speech-core/runtime-api.js"
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the npm runtime facade contract treated `speech-core/runtime-api.js` as an always-allowed bundled extension public surface, but `speech-core` is packaged as `packages/speech-core` and exposed through `plugin-sdk/tts-runtime`, not as `dist/extensions/speech-core/runtime-api.js`.
- Solution: remove `speech-core` from bundled-extension always-allowed runtime facade lists and add release verification that checks every always-allowed runtime facade has a packaged runtime sidecar.
- What changed: updated facade activation checks, QA runtime staging fixtures, and postpublish verification coverage for always-allowed bundled runtime facades.
- What did NOT change: Not changed / out of scope: `plugin-sdk/tts-runtime` still exports TTS through `../../packages/speech-core/runtime-api.js`; TTS API shape, plugin SDK export names, Gateway auth/provider behavior, package versioning, and broad missing-facade fallback behavior are unchanged.
- Why it matters / User impact: installed gateways no longer try to resolve a non-existent bundled extension surface for `speech-core/runtime-api.js`, avoiding the reported pre-provider failure path.
- Fixes #89425

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Installed package runtime facade verification and installed Gateway startup/RPC no longer surface `speech-core/runtime-api.js` as a missing bundled plugin public surface.
- **Real environment tested:** Local npm tarball installed globally into a temporary prefix from this branch, using a temporary HOME/config/state directory, a token-authenticated foreground Gateway on a random loopback port, and authenticated health/status/agent identity RPCs.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `daily_fix.sh bounded-live-proof --evidence "$EVIDENCE_DIR/installed-gateway-proof.md" --timeout 600 --grace 10 -- bash "$EVIDENCE_DIR/installed-gateway-proof.sh" "$EVIDENCE_DIR/installed-gateway-proof.md"`
  - Inside that proof: build output was packed with `npm pack --ignore-scripts`, installed with `npm install -g --prefix <temp> <tarball>`, the installed `openclaw` binary started a token-authenticated foreground Gateway on a random loopback port, then the installed binary called Gateway `health`, `status`, and `agent.identity.get` over that live Gateway.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - `installed-gateway-proof.md` captured a local tarball install where the installed package facade verifier returned `[]`, Gateway health returned `ok: true`, Gateway `status` returned `runtimeVersion: "2026.6.2"`, `agent.identity.get` returned the dev agent identity, and the Gateway log showed `agent runtime plugins pre-warmed` without `speech-core/runtime-api.js` or `Unable to resolve bundled plugin public surface`.
  - The installed package facade verifier also returned `[]` against the built tree before packaging.
- **Observed result after fix:** Runtime facade allowlist contains only bundled runtime extensions with packaged sidecars; `speech-core` is rejected as a bundled extension public surface when no bundled plugin manifest exists, while the installed Gateway starts and serves authenticated RPCs from the tarball.

  Copied live output from `installed-gateway-proof.md`:

  ```text
  Installed package facade verifier: []
  Gateway health: "ok": true, "plugins": { "errors": [] }
  Gateway status RPC: "runtimeVersion": "2026.6.2"
  Agent identity RPC: "agentId": "dev", "name": "C3-PO"
  Gateway log: agent runtime plugins pre-warmed in 15ms
  Runtime facade assertion: no speech-core/runtime-api.js and no Unable to resolve bundled plugin public surface
  ```
- **What was not tested:** External model-provider completion was not exercised; the issue fails before provider invocation, so the proof targets installed package facade resolution and Gateway startup/RPC behavior.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Fixed ClawSweeper's `speech-core` facade/package contract concern by removing `speech-core` from bundled extension always-allowed facade handling instead of adding a broad missing-surface fallback. Evidence: `src/plugin-sdk/facade-runtime.test.ts` now asserts `speech-core/runtime-api.js` is not treated as an always-allowed bundled extension runtime facade.
- **RF-002:** Added release-gate coverage for the tarball contract. Evidence: `test/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.test.ts` covers missing sidecars for always-allowed runtime facades, and `collectInstalledPackageErrors` calls `collectInstalledAlwaysAllowedRuntimeFacadeErrors`.

## Regression Test Plan
- **Target test file:** `src/plugin-sdk/facade-runtime.test.ts`, `src/tts/tts.test.ts`, `test/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.test.ts`, and `extensions/qa-lab/src/gateway-child.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `speech-core/runtime-api.js` is not an always-allowed bundled extension facade, TTS still resolves through `packages/speech-core`, and postpublish verification rejects always-allowed runtime facades without packaged sidecars.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** It covers the facade contract at the resolver boundary, the TTS public export path, the npm release verifier, and the QA staging path that previously mirrored the incorrect always-staged runtime list.

## Merge risk
- **Risk labels considered:** compatibility / availability / automation.
- **Risk explanation:** The change removes `speech-core` from bundled-extension facade activation and QA staging assumptions, which could affect any caller incorrectly importing it as a bundled extension runtime sidecar.
- **Why acceptable:** `speech-core` is not packaged as a bundled extension sidecar; the supported runtime path remains `plugin-sdk/tts-runtime` to `packages/speech-core/runtime-api.js`, and tests lock in both sides of that contract.

## Public contract / scope boundary
- **Contract surface:** The canonical public contract remains `plugin-sdk/tts-runtime` -> `packages/speech-core/runtime-api.js`; `dist/extensions/speech-core/runtime-api.js` is not a packaged bundled-extension public contract surface.
- **Compatibility/default behavior:** Existing TTS callers keep the same supported runtime export path. The only default behavior changed is that the bundled-extension facade resolver no longer grants always-allowed access to a sidecar path that is not packaged.
- **Related open PR scan:** daily-fix public-contract scan found no related open PRs for this same-contract correction; evidence file `public-contract-related-prs.json` has `related_open_prs: []` with `scan_error: null`.
- **Out of scope / not changed:** Provider execution, Gateway auth, TTS API shape, plugin SDK export names, npm versioning, and any broad missing-runtime fallback are intentionally left out of scope.

## Root Cause
- **Root cause:** Root contract source mismatch: the facade resolver source-of-truth allowlist classified `speech-core` as a bundled runtime extension because it was grouped with sidecar-backed bundled runtimes even though the npm package only ships it as `packages/speech-core` and never ships `dist/extensions/speech-core/runtime-api.js`.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix changes the source-of-truth allowlist and release verification contract so the invalid surface is no longer authorized and future allowlist entries must have matching packaged sidecars.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch quality warning context: no fallback, keyword filtering, or silent ignore path was added; the parser `return null` and `catch` paths are release-verifier parsing failures that become explicit package verification errors, while invalid runtime facade access still fails at the facade activation boundary as a non-bundled plugin surface.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** `src/plugin-sdk/facade-activation-check.runtime.ts` owns bundled facade activation decisions; `scripts/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.ts` now verifies the installed package projection matches that allowlist before release validation passes.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
